import requests
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, trim, lit, upper, when
import nest_asyncio
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType
from playwright.async_api import async_playwright
import agentql 
import json
import asyncio

fully_open_access = ["TACO","THRI","TOPLAS","TOPML","JDS","COLA","CACM","DGOV","DTRAP","FAC","ICPS","PACMPL","PACMSE"]

default_apc = 1800
Year = 2024

kbart_temp_path = 'abfss://apc@dloadatdl.dfs.core.windows.net/bronze/Association for Computing Machinery (ACM)/kbart/*.txt'
silver_path = 'abfss://apc@dloadatdl.dfs.core.windows.net/silver/Association for Computing Machinery (ACM)/all/'

def populate_oajournal_df_from_json(input_data):
    # Ensure input is a dictionary
    if isinstance(input_data, str):
        data = json.loads(input_data)  # Parse JSON string
    elif isinstance(input_data, dict):
        data = input_data  # Use as is
    else:
        raise TypeError("Input must be a JSON string or a dictionary")
    # Extract the journal data
    journals = data.get("open_access_titles", [])
    journal_data = []
    journal_article_list_price = data.get("Open_Access_Pricing", {}).get("journal_article_list_price", None)
    
    for journal in journals:
        name = journal["name"]
        # Extract the title_id from the text within parentheses
        title_id = name[name.find("(")+1 : name.find(")")]
        journal_data.append((name, title_id, journal_article_list_price))
    
    # Define the schema
    schema = StructType([
        StructField("name", StringType(), True),
        StructField("title_id", StringType(), True),
        StructField("apc", StringType(), True)
    ])
    
    # Create a Spark DataFrame
    df = spark.createDataFrame(journal_data, schema=schema)
    return df

import os
os.environ["AGENTQL_API_KEY"]='Gqq-9anMwmlM2Bz8HO_CqXQl4gEkauGNqBmAc6x4EU12gH-gPkzIig'
URL = 'https://www.acm.org/publications/openaccess'
# Apply nest_asyncio to allow nested event loops (important in Jupyter notebooks)
nest_asyncio.apply()
search_query = """
{
    open_access_titles[] {
        name
    }
    Open_Access_Pricing {
        journal_article_list_price
    }
}
"""

async def fetch_oa_journal_data(url: str, search_query: str):
    async with async_playwright() as playwright:
        browser = await playwright.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        page = await agentql.wrap_async(browser.new_page())
        await page.goto(url)
        print(url)
        # Query the page for ownership data
        response = await page.query_data(search_query)
        print(response)
        return response

response = asyncio.run(fetch_oa_journal_data(URL, search_query))
oa_journal_df = populate_oajournal_df_from_json(response)
oa_journal_df.show()

def extract_oa_journal_spark_df(input_string):
    # Parse the input string as JSON
    data = json.loads(input_string)
    
    # Extract the journal data
    journals = data.get("open_access_titles", [])
    journal_data = []
    
    for journal in journals:
        name = journal["name"]
        # Extract the title_id from the text within parentheses
        title_id = name[name.find("(")+1 : name.find(")")]
        journal_data.append((name, title_id))
    
    # Define the schema
    schema = StructType([
        StructField("name", StringType(), True),
        StructField("title_id", StringType(), True)
    ])
    
    # Create a Spark DataFrame
    df = spark.createDataFrame(journal_data, schema=schema)
    return df

# Step 1: Read the file into a PySpark DataFrame, specifying the delimiter as '\t'
kbart_df = spark.read.option("delimiter", "\t").csv(kbart_temp_path, header=True, inferSchema=True)

# Remove rows where publication_type is not "serial".
# Ensure last_date_online is blank (null).
# Ensure publisher is not "ACM".

filtered_kbart_df = kbart_df.filter(
    (col("publication_type") == "Serial") &
    (col("publisher_name") == "Association for Computing Machinery") &
    ((trim(col("date_last_issue_online")).isNull()) | (trim(col("date_last_issue_online")) == ""))
)

new_df = filtered_kbart_df.select(
    lit("Association for Computing Machinery (ACM)").alias("Publisher"),
    col("print_identifier").alias("ISSN1"),
    col("online_identifier").alias("ISSN2"),
    col("publication_title").alias("Title"),
    lit("Publisher Website via Misc Publisher APCs list").alias("Source"),
    #lit("").alias("Model"),
    when(upper(col("title_id")).isin(fully_open_access), "Fully OA").otherwise("Hybrid").alias("Model"),
    lit(default_apc).alias("APC"),
    lit("USD").alias("Currency"),
    lit("").alias("License Types"),
    lit(Year).alias("Year"),
    col("title_id").alias("PublisherID"),
    col("title_url").alias("Publisher URL"),
    col("publisher_name").alias("Society")
